key_insight: A lightweight MobileNetV3 classifier can detect multiple pipeline weld defect classes with very high accuracy, even on noisy RGB images from different capture conditions.
engineering_relevance: The approach supports fast, low-power, on-device inspection for pipeline welding quality control, making automated defect screening more practical in field deployments.
method: Preprocess weld images and apply data augmentation to improve robustness.
Train and compare MobileNetV3-Small and MobileNetV3-Large for multi-class defect classification.
Evaluate performance on two real-world datasets collected with HDR cameras and a mobile phone under uncontrolled conditions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 99.99% accuracy on the 4,927-image AI5083 dataset and improved performance on the 78-image PWDID dataset, with about a 3% gain over prior methods.
watch_item: The very small PWDID dataset and noisy acquisition conditions suggest that broader external validation is still needed before deployment in diverse industrial environments.
paper_title: MobileNetV3 for Pipeline Welding Defect Detection: A Computer Vision Approach on Noisy RGB Images
